title: Power BI - Mobile-Optimized Reporting
applies_to: Optimizing Power BI reports for iOS and Android apps

confirmed_facts.fact: Power BI mobile-optimized views display in the Power BI mobile apps for iOS and Android when a report page has a mobile layout and the phone is in portrait orientation.
Reports viewed through a web browser always display in the standard, non-optimized view rather than the phone-optimized mobile layout.
Mobile layout view is available in both Power BI Desktop and the Power BI service and supports dragging visuals to a phone canvas, auto-create layout generation, grid controls, and layout switching.

Microsoft recommends building desktop and mobile layouts together, focusing on the most important content, avoiding side-by-side visuals except for simple elements, and using standard mobile visual sizes.
Mobile-optimized report pages are indicated with a special icon in the Power BI mobile apps.

implementation_notes: Treat mobile layout as a distinct design surface, not a scaled-down desktop page.
Prioritize a short top-to-bottom narrative for mobile users and remove low-value visuals from the mobile view.
Use auto-create mobile layout as a starting point, then refine for readability and interaction quality.
If the audience is primarily mobile, bake mobile layout decisions into initial report design instead of adding them at the end.
caveats: Mobile layout benefits only Power BI mobile apps, not browser-based viewing.
A report can be technically mobile-optimized and still perform poorly if it carries too many heavy visuals.
Mobile usability and desktop usability often require different layout decisions.
